title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During an interrogation, an unnamed woman denies attempting to poison Marina Gregg or kill Mrs. Badcock. Despite the Inspector's suggestion that she intended to kill Marina, she claims to know nothing about the culprit. She sarcastically suggests that several people could be hiding their true identities, including a black-haired secretary, Mr. Blackley Preston, various servants, or employees of the movie company.

At 16 Aubrey Street, Shelley Beck discusses her frustrations with her husband, Jim Beck, while he assembles a model. Shelley expresses resentment toward a neighbor, Miss Knight—whom she refers to as Miss Maple—for meddling in her business and offering unsolicited dietary advice. She criticizes the atmosphere of the new housing estate, noting that neighbors such as the Hartwells and the Barnabys are constantly complaining or making noise. She also mentions the breakup of Lily Price and Harry, whom she considers a bad person.

The conversation shifts to local gossip regarding Arthur Badcock and Mary Bain; Shelley suggests that Arthur's current state of mind may not be genuine, though Jim warns her that his wife has just died. Annoyed by Jim's lack of attention, Shelley decides to go to Glady Dixon's house to borrow a dress. After leaving her home, she passes a neighbor's house and heads toward number sixteen on Blenheim Lane, where she enters an open door and calls for Glady.
